name: Monstera Deliciosa
description: Architectural leaves and a sculptural silhouette make this a signature plant for modern interiors.

care.light: Thrives in bright, indirect light with gentle morning sun.
care.watering: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry, then drain thoroughly.
care.commonIssues: Wipe leaves monthly, rotate weekly, and watch for drooping caused by inconsistent watering.

name: Indoor Olive Tree
description: A refined, silvery-green tree that brings warmth and height to calm, sunlit rooms.

care.light: Needs a bright spot with several hours of direct sun.
care.watering: Allow the soil to dry slightly between waterings.
care.commonIssues: Yellowing leaves often point to overwatering or too little direct sun.

name: Calathea Orbifolia
description: Broad patterned foliage with soft silver bands for a lush, gallery-like feel.

care.light: Prefers medium to bright indirect light.
care.watering: Keep soil lightly moist and use filtered water when possible.
care.commonIssues: Brown edges can appear if the air is too dry or the water quality is harsh.

name: Ficus Altissima
description: Variegated leaves and an airy shape designed to anchor a room with understated drama.

care.light: Bright filtered light keeps the variegation crisp.
care.watering: Water when the upper layer of soil dries out.
care.commonIssues: Leaf drop is common after sudden moves, drafts, or irregular watering.
